body:
For most homeowners, **hiring a professional is the safer choice** , especially if your garage door uses **torsion springs** (the springs mounted above the door). These springs store a large amount of energy, and a mistake during removal or winding can cause severe injuries. Home improvement experts consistently describe torsion spring replacement as one of the more hazardous home repairs. [Can You Change Out Garage Door Springs Yourself?](https://www.bobvila.com/articles/replace-a-garage-door-spring/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[How to Replace a Garage Door Spring - This Old House](https://www.thisoldhouse.com/garages/replacing-door-springs?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

If you have a broken spring:

  * Don't keep operating the door if possible. A broken spring can overload the opener or cause the door to move unpredictably. [How Much Does It Actually Cost to Replace a Garage Door Spring?](https://www.thespruce.com/garage-door-spring-replacement-8635322?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[How to Replace a Garage Door Spring - This Old House](https://www.thisoldhouse.com/garages/replacing-door-springs?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * Avoid trying to wind or unwind torsion springs without the proper tools and experience.

  * It's reasonable to call a garage door technician for diagnosis and replacement.

Regarding **[Valiant Garage Doors](https://valiantgaragedoor.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)** : if you're in their service area (Phoenix/Chandler, Arizona), they advertise spring replacement, same-day service, and emergency repairs. Their business also has a recent [Better Business Bureau profile](https://www.bbb.org/us/ca/pleasanton/profile/garage-door-repair/valiant-garage-door-1116-980936?utm_source=chatgpt.com) with an A+ accreditation, and the BBB page currently shows positive customer reviews. [Valiant Garage Door | BBB Reviews | Better Business Bureau](https://www.bbb.org/us/ca/pleasanton/profile/garage-door-repair/valiant-garage-door-1116-980936/customer-reviews?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

If you're **not** in the Phoenix area, Valiant likely isn't the right option due to location. From your apparent location, a nearby garage door company such as Patriot Door Service may be a more practical choice.

If you tell me:

  * whether your door has **torsion** or **extension** springs (or upload a photo), and

  * roughly where you're located,

I can give more specific advice on the repair and help you determine whether a professional is the better option in your situation.
